title: Fruit Ceramic Planter
description:
In Italian we call it a cachepot — a decorative pot you slip a plant into. Our artisans in Vietri sul Mare shape this one on the wheel and paint the entire fruit design freehand before firing. The result is a solid, glossy ceramic planter that works indoors or outdoors.

The body is covered in a dense arrangement of hand-painted fruit against a deep cobalt blue background: fat yellow lemons with green leaves, bunches of blue grapes, red apples, halved pomegranates showing their seeds, and sliced oranges. There is a lot going on, and it is all painted with confident, detailed brushwork. The rim has a yellow band with a scrolling vine border, and the base is finished with matching yellow stripes. The overall effect is rich and full — this is not a minimalist piece.

At 20 cm, it is a good size for a small herb plant, a flowering pot, or even just sitting empty on a table as a decorative bowl. It is also available in 30 cm and 35 cm if you need something larger — just send us a request.

Each cachepot is painted by hand, so no two will have the fruit arranged in exactly the same way. The ceramic is sturdy and the glaze protects well against moisture.
excerpt: A ceramic planter densely painted with fruit on cobalt blue, 20 cm. Handmade in Vietri sul Mare. Also available in larger sizes.
seoTitle: Fruit Ceramic Planter — Ceramiche Da Mario
seoDescription: Handmade ceramic cachepot with fruit on cobalt blue, 20 cm. Painted freehand in Vietri sul Mare. Ceramiche Da Mario, Praiano.
